---
title: "Plots for party trees"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Plots for party trees}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r}
library(ineqTrees)
```

<!-- WARNING - This vignette is generated by {fusen} from dev/plot_tree_helpers.Rmd: do not edit by hand -->



<!--
 You need to run the 'description' chunk in the '0-dev_history.Rmd' file before continuing your code there.
-->





# plot helpers





```{r example-tree_compact_split_label}
tree_compact_split_label("wealth_index", "lowest")

tree_compact_split_label(
  "water_source",
  "piped, borehole, well, river",
  var_labels = c(water_source = "Water source"),
  plural_overrides = c("Water source" = "sources")
)
```







```{r example-tree_edge_panel_compact}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ctree_ci(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

inherits(tree_edge_panel_compact, "grapcon_generator")
is.function(tree_edge_panel_compact(fit))
```








```{r example-tree_inner_panel_labeled}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ctree_ci(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

inherits(tree_inner_panel_labeled, "grapcon_generator")
is.function(tree_inner_panel_labeled(fit))
```





```{r example-tree_build_terminal_stats}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ctree_ci(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

tree_build_terminal_stats(
  fit,
  toy_data,
  stat_funs = list(
    n = nrow,
    mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
  )
)
```




```{r example-tree_terminal_panel_stats}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ctree_ci(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

stats_df <- tree_build_terminal_stats(
  fit,
  toy_data,
  stat_funs = list(
    n = nrow,
    mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
  )
)

inherits(tree_terminal_panel_stats, "grapcon_generator")
is.function(tree_terminal_panel_stats(fit, stats_df))
```



```{r}
#' Compute terminal node means for a fitted tree, with optional observation
#'  weights
#' predict_ctree_ci_terminal_mean()` computes mean outcome values 
#' for each terminal
#' node of a fitted tree by assigning training observations to nodes and then
#' calculating weighted means within each node. The function can be used to
#' generate predictions for new data based on terminal node means, with a
#' global mean fallback for unseen nodes.
#' @param fit A fitted tree object inheriting from `party`.
#' @param train_data A data frame used to assign observations to terminal
#'   nodes and compute node means.
#' @param new_data A data frame for which to generate predictions. Defaults to
#'   `train_data`.
#' @param outcome_name A character scalar naming the outcome variable in
#'   `train_data`.
#' @param weights An optional numeric vector of observation weights used
#'   when computing node means. Defaults to equal weights.
#' @importFrom data.table as.data.table copy `:=`
predict_ctree_ci_terminal_mean <- function(fit,
                                           train_data,
                                           new_data = train_data,
                                           outcome_name,
                                           weights = NULL) {
  train_dt <- data.table::as.data.table(train_data)
  new_dt <- data.table::as.data.table(new_data)

  if (is.null(weights)) {
    weights <- rep(1, nrow(train_dt))
  }

  train_dt <- data.table::copy(train_dt)
  train_dt[, `:=`(
    .node_id__ = predict(fit, newdata = train_data, type = "node")
  )]
  train_dt[, `:=`(.weight__ = weights)]
  train_dt[, `:=`(.outcome__ = as.numeric(get(outcome_name)))]

  node_mean_dt <- train_dt[
    ,
    .(.node_mean__ = stats::weighted.mean(.outcome__, .weight__, na.rm = TRUE)),
    by = .node_id__
  ]

  global_mean <- stats::weighted.mean(
    train_dt$.outcome__, weights, na.rm = TRUE)
  new_nodes <- predict(fit, newdata = new_dt, type = "node")
  pred <- node_mean_dt[match(new_nodes, .node_id__), .node_mean__]
  pred[is.na(pred)] <- global_mean
  as.numeric(pred)
}
```





```{r example-plot_ci_tree}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ctree_ci(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = ci_tree_control(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, maxdepth = 1)
)

plot(fit)
plot(
  fit,
  data = toy_data,
  terminal_stats = list(
    n = nrow,
    mean_outcome = function(df) mean(df$outcome)
  )
)
```
